core_behavior: The system must automatically send a notification to a user when their order transitions to a 'shipped' status.
The notification must inform the user that their order has been dispatched and provide relevant tracking and order details.
The notification must be sent exactly once per shipment event per order (or per shipment if an order contains multiple shipments).
The system must detect or receive the order-shipped event.
The system must retrieve the relevant user contact information and order details.
The system must compose a notification message using a defined template.
The system must deliver the notification via one or more configured channels.
The system must record the outcome of the notification attempt.
inputs: The notification is triggered by an order status change event where the new status equals SHIPPED.
The triggering event may originate from an internal order management system (OMS) publishing a state-change event (e.g., via message queue or webhook).
The triggering event may originate from a direct API call from a fulfillment or warehouse system.
The triggering event may originate from a scheduled polling job that detects status changes in the orders database.
The event payload must include order_id (string UUID): Unique identifier of the order.
The event payload must include shipment_id (string UUID): Unique identifier of the shipment (supports partial shipments).
The event payload must include shipped_at (ISO 8601 datetime): Timestamp when the order was marked as shipped.
The event payload must include user_id (string UUID): Identifier of the user who placed the order.
The event payload must include carrier (string): Name of the shipping carrier (e.g., 'FedEx', 'UPS').
The event payload must include tracking_number (string): Carrier-assigned tracking number.
The event payload must include tracking_url (string URL): Deep link to carrier tracking page.
The event payload must include estimated_delivery_date (date ISO 8601): Estimated delivery date (optional but recommended).
The event payload must include items_shipped (array of objects): List of items included in this shipment.
The system retrieves email address from the user profile service.
The system retrieves phone number (for SMS) from the user profile service.
The system retrieves push notification device tokens from the user profile service.
The system retrieves preferred notification channel(s) from the user profile service.
The system retrieves preferred language/locale from the user profile service.
The system retrieves notification opt-in/opt-out status per channel from the user profile service.
outputs: The system must support Email delivery channel in HTML + plain-text fallback format; required (default).
The system must support SMS delivery channel in plain text, ≤160 characters per segment format; optional.
The system must support Push Notification delivery channel with title + body + deep link payload format; optional.
The system must support In-App Notification delivery channel as a structured message stored in notification inbox; required (always).
The channel(s) used are determined by user preferences and opt-in status.
Email subject must be: Your order #[order_id_short] has shipped!
Email body must include a greeting with user's first name.
Email body must include confirmation that the order has shipped.
Email body must include order ID (display-friendly short form).
Email body must include list of items shipped.
Email body must include carrier name and tracking number.
Email body must include clickable tracking URL.
Email body must include estimated delivery date (if available).
Email body must include link to order detail page.
Email body must include customer support contact information.
Email body must include unsubscribe/manage preferences link.
SMS body example: Your order #[order_id_short] has shipped via [carrier]. Track it: [tracking_url] Est. delivery: [date]. Reply STOP to opt out.
Push notification title: Order Shipped 🚚
Push notification body: Your order #[order_id_short] is on its way! Est. delivery: [date].
Push notification deep link: Opens order detail screen in mobile app.
In-App notification is a full structured record stored in the user's notification inbox.
In-App notification is marked as unread on creation.
In-App notification includes all fields from the email body in structured form.
The system must record the outcome of the notification attempt as an audit/delivery record.
edge_cases: An order may contain multiple shipments; the notification must be sent exactly once per shipment event per shipment.
estimated_delivery_date is optional; email body and push notification body include it only if available.
performance_constraints: SMS messages must be ≤160 characters per segment.
